content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Iteration 3 (stability check): no source changes required. The iteration-1 implementation continues to satisfy every acceptance criterion. Verified the diff scope is unchanged (4 files: src/adapters/process_backend.rs +354/-187 net, src/contexts/workflow_composition/payloads.rs -140 unused interim helper + tests, tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs +206 new regression tests, tests/unit/workflow_panels_test.rs -81 obsolete codex schema enum test; total +402/-379) and re-ran cargo fmt --check (clean), cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings (Finished `dev` profile, no warnings), and the four new regression tests (codex_execution_stage_args_do_not_include_output_schema, codex_execution_stage_synthesizes_payload_and_copies_transcript, codex_execution_stage_synthesizes_payload_with_fallback_when_last_message_missing, claude_execution_stage_still_passes_json_schema — all pass). The nix build artifact from iteration 1 (result/bin/ralph-burning, static, 19,457,872 bytes) remains valid since no source files changed since.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Confirm git diff scope unchanged from iteration 1 (4 source files modified, .ralph-burning/ untracked and out of review scope).
2. [Completed] Re-run cargo fmt --check (clean) and cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings (Finished, no warnings).
3. [Completed] Re-run the four codex/claude execution-stage regression tests; all four pass (3 codex + 1 claude).
4. [Skipped] No additional code edits this iteration — the implementation has converged at iteration 1.

## Validation Evidence

- git diff --stat src/ tests/: 4 files changed, 402 insertions(+), 379 deletions(-) — unchanged from iteration 1
- cargo fmt --check: clean (no diff output)
- cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings: clean (Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 0.13s)
- cargo test --features test-stub --locked --test unit codex_execution_stage: 3 passed; 0 failed
- cargo test --features test-stub --locked --test unit claude_execution_stage: 1 passed; 0 failed

## Outstanding Risks

- Real-world rollout depends on codex emitting the assistant's last natural-language message into --output-last-message when --output-schema is absent. If a future codex CLI release stops writing that file in this mode, the synth path falls back to the deterministic 'codex implementation turn completed (exit 0); see transcript' summary — work still proceeds and the forward-progress invariant holds, but change_summary loses the model's prose.
- The transcript copy uses request.invocation_id (which encodes run_id-stage-cycle-round) for the filename rather than the explicit dashed format from the prompt's hint. Any external tooling expecting the spelled-out format will need to parse invocation_id instead.
